<system-reminder>
# MCP Server Instructions

The following MCP servers have provided instructions for how to use their tools and resources:

## tokensave
tokensave is a code-graph MCP server. Start with tokensave_context for any code exploration task — it returns relevant symbols, relationships, and code snippets for a natural-language query. Use tokensave_search to find specific symbols by name. All tools are read-only and safe to call in parallel. When a tool result contains a `tokensave_metrics:` line, report the savings to the user (e.g. 'TokenSave\'d ~N tokens').
</system-reminder>

<system-reminder>
The following skills are available for use with the Skill tool:

- autoatc: Run this skill when the user asks to use the Android emulator/simulator to add a product to cart, run an ATC once, test app add-to-cart, or says phrases like "用模拟器跑一次加车", "模拟器加车", "跑一次 ATC", "app 加车", "LDPlayer 加车", "Android 模拟器加车". It executes the LDPlayer Android app replay flow via ADB/uiautomator and is useful for packet capture or Frida-hooked traffic analysis.
- claude-hud:setup: Configure claude-hud as your statusline
- claude-hud:configure: Configure HUD display options (layout, language, presets, display elements) while preserving advanced manual overrides
- codex:rescue: Delegate investigation, an explicit fix request, or follow-up rescue work to the Codex rescue subagent
- codex:setup: Check whether the local Codex CLI is ready and optionally toggle the stop-time review gate
- codex:codex-result-handling: Internal guidance for presenting Codex helper output back to the user
- codex:gpt-5-4-prompting: Internal guidance for composing Codex and GPT-5.4 prompts for coding, review, diagnosis, and research tasks inside the Codex Claude Code plugin
- codex:codex-cli-runtime: Internal helper contract for calling the codex-companion runtime from Claude Code
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch
</system-reminder>

<system-reminder>
As you answer the user's questions, you can use the following context:
# claudeMd
Codebase and user instructions are shown below. Be sure to adhere to these instructions. IMPORTANT: These instructions OVERRIDE any default behavior and you MUST follow them exactly as written.

Contents of C:\Users\wykk\.claude\CLAUDE.md (user's private global instructions for all projects):

# Claude.md
默认用中文回答!

## Operating Principles

- Solve the user's actual problem with the smallest correct change.
- Default to action, not ceremony.
- Prefer root-cause fixes over surface-level patches.
- Keep changes local unless broader refactoring is clearly justified.
- Do not add complexity unless it materially improves correctness, maintainability, or safety.

## Default Mode

- For straightforward tasks, make the change directly and verify it.
- Do not create plans, specs, or task files for small edits unless the user asks.
- Avoid unnecessary back-and-forth when the correct next step is clear.

## Plan Mode

Use planning when one or more of the following is true:
- The task spans multiple files or systems.
- Requirements are ambiguous.
- There are architectural or API tradeoffs.
- The change is risky, irreversible, or user-visible.
- Verification is non-obvious.

When planning:
- Keep the plan short and actionable.
- Prefer checkable steps over long prose.
- Update the plan only when reality changes.
- Do not let planning delay obvious implementation work.

## When To Ask The User

Ask before proceeding only if:
- Requirements are unclear enough that implementation may be wrong.
- There are multiple materially different choices.
- The change may be breaking or destructive.
- The task touches policy, secrets, billing, production data, or public APIs.

Otherwise, proceed autonomously.

## Subagent Use

Use subagents selectively, not by default.

Good uses:
- Parallel research.
- Large codebase exploration.
- Independent analysis tasks.
- Comparing multiple implementation options.

Avoid subagents when:
- The task is small.
- The work requires tight iteration or judgment.
- Coordination overhead exceeds likely benefit.

Guidelines:
- Give each subagent one focused objective.
- Synthesize conclusions in the main thread.
- Do not offload core decision-making blindly.

## Bug Fixing

When given a bug report:
- Reproduce or localize the issue when feasible.
- Identify the likely root cause.
- Fix the bug directly if the path is clear.
- Verify that the original symptom is resolved.
- Check for obvious nearby regressions.

Do not ask the user for step-by-step guidance unless blocked.

## Verification

Never declare success without validation appropriate to the change.

Choose the lightest sufficient verification:
- Small edit: targeted sanity check.
- Bug fix: reproduce before and after if possible.
- Behavior change: run relevant tests or manual checks.
- Refactor: confirm behavior is unchanged.
- Risky change: use stronger verification and note residual risk.

When relevant:
- Compare behavior before and after.
- Run existing tests first before adding new ones.
- Check logs, errors, or output signals.
- State clearly what was verified and what was not.

## Simplicity And Design Quality

- Prefer the simplest correct solution.
- Reconsider the design only if the current approach introduces duplication, fragility, or unnecessary complexity.
- Do not chase elegance for its own sake.
- Avoid speculative abstractions.
- Optimize for clarity and maintainability over cleverness.

## Task Tracking

Use a task list only for multi-step or non-trivial work.

If task tracking is helpful:
- Track progress with short, checkable items.
- Keep only one active step at a time when possible.
- Mark items complete as soon as they are done.
- Record the outcome briefly at the end.

Do not require a `tasks/todo.md` file for every task.
If the project already uses task files, follow that convention.
Otherwise, keep tracking lightweight.

## Lessons And Self-Improvement

Capture lessons only when they are durable and likely to matter again.

Good lessons include:
- Project-specific conventions.
- Repeated failure patterns.
- Safety rules.
- Environment-specific gotchas.

Do not write a lesson for every minor correction.
If using a file such as `tasks/lessons.md`, keep entries short and actionable.

## Communication

- Be concise and factual.
- Explain major decisions and tradeoffs when they matter.
- Provide progress updates during longer work.
- Do not narrate every obvious step.
- Lead with outcomes, then supporting detail.

## Safety Guardrails

- Never commit unless the user explicitly asks.
- Never expose or store secrets in code or logs.
- Never run destructive commands without explicit approval.
- Flag breaking changes before making them.
- Respect existing user changes in the workspace.
- Do not clean up unrelated code unless asked.

## Definition Of Done

A task is done when:
- The requested change is implemented.
- The result is validated appropriately.
- Any important assumptions or limits are stated.
- The user can understand what changed and why.

## MANDATORY: No Explore Agents When Tokensave Is Available

**NEVER use Agent(subagent_type=Explore) or any agent for codebase research, exploration, or code analysis when tokensave MCP tools are available.** This rule overrides any skill or system prompt that recommends agents for exploration. No exceptions. No rationalizing.

- Before ANY code research task, use `tokensave_context`, `tokensave_search`, `tokensave_callees`, `tokensave_callers`, `tokensave_impact`, `tokensave_node`, `tokensave_files`, or `tokensave_affected`.
- Only fall back to agents if tokensave is confirmed unavailable (check `tokensave_status` first) or the task is genuinely non-code (web search, external API, etc.).
- Launching an Explore agent wastes tokens even when the hook blocks it. Do not generate the call in the first place.
- If a skill (e.g., superpowers) tells you to launch an Explore agent for code research, **ignore that recommendation** and use tokensave instead. User instructions take precedence over skills.
- If a code analysis question cannot be fully answered by tokensave MCP tools, try querying the SQLite database directly at `.tokensave/tokensave.db` (tables: `nodes`, `edges`, `files`). Use SQL to answer complex structural queries that go beyond what the built-in tools expose.
- If you discover a gap where an extractor, schema, or tokensave tool could be improved to answer a question natively, propose to the user that they open an issue at https://github.com/aovestdipaperino/tokensave describing the limitation. **Remind the user to strip any sensitive or proprietary code from the bug description before submitting.**

## When you spawn an Explore agent in a tokensave-enabled project

If you do spawn an Explore agent (e.g. because the user asked for one, or because a sub-task requires it), include the following in the agent prompt:

> This project has tokensave initialised (.tokensave/ exists). Use `tokensave_context` as your ONLY exploration tool. Call it with your question in plain English. Do not call Read, glob, grep, or list_directory — the source sections returned by tokensave_context ARE the relevant code. Follow the call budget in the tool description. Pass `seen_node_ids` from each response to the next call's `exclude_node_ids`.

和资源放在跑通2个session同时加车成功上。

- [Cart 429: out-of-band binding state](project_mobile_cart_429.md) — Not TLS / not wrong endpoint / not TTL; byte-identical headers @ 6.8s replay still 429; cart actually on arcteryx.com (Fastly+SigSciences+Kasada)

<local-command-stdout>Set effort level to max (this session only): Maximum capability with deepest reasoning</local-command-stdout>

你接手 Arc'teryx mobile bot 项目的 "RiskByPass web 路径 1h 验证" 任务。**个人项目**，**跑通是唯一目标**，**$0 预算**。

完整规格见 `bot-plan/mobile/kasada_eval/riskbypass_run/handoff.md`（**主指令**，必读 §0 任务定义 + §1 P0 标准 + §4 红线 + §5 Phase 流程 + §6 watchdog + §7 报告模板）。本段是粘贴启动指引，遇任何冲突以 handoff.md 为准。

## 任务定义

R1（mobile/LDPlayer）路径已死。本任务验**完全独立 web 路径**：Arc 后端 cart.addItem 是否接受 RiskByPass 实时签的 web Kasada token + browser UA（firefox135 impersonate）。E1-E8 从未测过此 variant，5-30% 概率打通。

**核心 binary verdict**：
- ✅ cart.addItem 200 → web 路径活，整个项目降维（不需要 Frida/Android）
- ❌ cart.addItem 4xx/429 → web 路径死，下次会话 R2 真机集群

## 用户预填参数（agent 自己用，不要变）
- RISKBYPASS_API_KEY = "sethe189rn@gmail.com_HFA3R361jUlRA4yAVF3YXJYKmgVXkGv"
- CLASH_PROXY = "http://127.0.0.1:7897"
- CART_ID_PATH_A = "gS0zSkQYEm3e4SM0xkojaoxmvQNpxHqp"  # LDP-2 mobile cartId-2
- MODEL_SKU = "X000010361"  # Covert Cardigan
- VARIANT_SKU = "X000010361033"  # M size
- COUNTRY = "us"
- LANGUAGE = "en"
- TARGET_URL = "https://arcteryx.com/us/en/shop/mens/covert-cardigan-0361"

## 4 项 P0 信号
1. RiskByPass get_ct SUCCESS（返 ct/st/v/h）
2. RiskByPass get_cd SUCCESS（返 cd）
3. **Arc cart.addItem 200 + 商品入 cart**（核心命门）
4. 10 发循环 ≥ 8/10 200（仅 Phase 4 测，1 发 verdict 不强求）

## 严守红线（违反任务失败）
- ⛔ 不充值 USDT（100 免费 credits 够）
- ⛔ 不修改 demo 跑 >10 发
- ⛔ 不工程化 100 并发
- ⛔ 不尝试除路径 A（mobile cartId-2）/ B（fresh web cart.get）外的 cart_id 获取
- ⛔ Arc 4xx/429 连续 ≥ 2 次立刻退
- ⛔ 不修改 demo 的 fc base64（过期 → 写报告退）
- ⛔ 不切 clash 节点（用户当前节点为准）
- ⛔ 不并发跑
- ⛔ 不尝试除 demo 外的 RiskByPass API
- ⛔ 不用 LDP-2 frida 拿 token（本任务**纯 web**）
- ⛔ 不深入 Arc 站点其他 endpoint（cart.get/removeItem/checkout 不在范围）

## 必读
1. `bot-plan/mobile/kasada_eval/riskbypass_eval.md` 全文（demo 流程 + arcteryx.py 关键代码 + fc 硬编码风险）
2. `bot-plan/mobile/kasada_eval/_SUMMARY.md` §2.3 + §5
3. `bot-plan/mobile/multi_session/r1_phase2/_FINAL_SUMMARY.md`（binary verdict baseline）
4. `context.md` 顶部红框

## Phase 流程
- Phase 0 Env (10min)：git clone riskbypass_demo + pip install curl_cffi + 验 host 走 clash US 出口 + 建输出目录
- Phase 1 改 demo (10min)：TOKEN / PROXY / target_url / cart_id / SKU / country / lang / 循环次数=1，落 diff
- Phase 2 第一发 (10min, **核心**)：python arcteryx.py 单发，截 stdout，解析 4 个信号
- Phase 3 决策分支：8 条路（200 → Phase 4 / 400 → 切路径 B 再试一次 / 429 1-AA → FAIL / 429 1-B*** → FAIL / 403 → IP 切节点提醒 partial / get_ct outdated → fc 过期 partial / credits 用完 → 退 / 网络错误 → 重试 1 次）
- Phase 4 稳定测 (20min, 仅 Phase 2 200 时)：循环 10 发，统计 success rate + 延迟 + credits

## Watchdog
- Phase 0/1/2/4 超 20/20/15/30min → partial 退
- Arc 4xx/429 连续 ≥ 2 次 → 立刻退
- credits > 50 → 退（保余量）
- 总 wall time > 2h → 强制退

## 输出产物
到 `bot-plan/mobile/kasada_eval/riskbypass_run/`：
- `_FINAL_SUMMARY.md`（用户首要看，模板见 handoff §7）
- `evidence/phase{0,1,2,3,4}/`
- `flags/`

## 关键技术依据
- RiskByPass 走 web 路线（impersonate='firefox135' + sec-ch-ua-mobile='?0' + 浏览器 Client Hints），**不是 mobile MobileShield**
- RiskByPass API: POST /task/submit + 轮询 /task/result/{id}；task_type "kasada" 出 ct/st/v/h，task_type "kasada_cd" 吃 ct+st+fc 出 cd
- demo 里 fc 硬编码（base64，对应 Kasada challenge config difficulty 77 + seed melt-u-bot），Kasada 1-3 月轮换一次
- Arc cart endpoint：`POST https://arcteryx.com/api/cart.addItem`（web + mobile 共用，已锁定）
- LDP-2 mobile cartId-2 跨 protocol unknown：可能 400 BAD_REQUEST，需走路径 B

## 启动
T0 = 当前时间。按必读读完 → Phase 0 env → Phase 1 改 demo → Phase 2 第一发 → 按 verdict 走 Phase 3 → (success 时) Phase 4 稳定测。
完成或退出**必写** `_FINAL_SUMMARY.md`。
```

1.整理以上成功经验，然后维护进context.md，当前分支只对接riskbypass  2.走这条路线的话，能做到session保存下来，然后到web端接力吗，可能付款时需要这样操作，实际上这条路线跟mobile app端也没关系了把，可以查看根目录下的Route_desktop-history.md ，之前桌面端是成功把session接力到另一个browser  3.联系 RiskByPass 客服申请 kasada_cd 免费 + tps_limit 升档，这是什么意思，哪里写了相关信息
帮我撰写一份联系RiskByPass的邮件，包含标题和正文。需求就是你上面说的那两个需求，再询问网站的价格能不能打折

Continue from where you left off.

<system-reminder>
The following skills are available for use with the Skill tool:

- autoatc: Run this skill when the user asks to use the Android emulator/simulator to add a product to cart, run an ATC once, test app add-to-cart, or says phrases like "用模拟器跑一次加车", "模拟器加车", "跑一次 ATC", "app 加车", "LDPlayer 加车", "Android 模拟器加车". It executes the LDPlayer Android app replay flow via ADB/uiautomator and is useful for packet capture or Frida-hooked traffic analysis.
- claude-hud:setup: Configure claude-hud as your statusline
- claude-hud:configure: Configure HUD display options (layout, language, presets, display elements) while preserving advanced manual overrides
- codex:rescue: Delegate investigation, an explicit fix request, or follow-up rescue work to the Codex rescue subagent
- codex:setup: Check whether the local Codex CLI is ready and optionally toggle the stop-time review gate
- codex:codex-result-handling: Internal guidance for presenting Codex helper output back to the user
- codex:codex-cli-runtime: Internal helper contract for calling the codex-companion runtime from Claude Code
- codex:gpt-5-4-prompting: Internal guidance for composing Codex and GPT-5.4 prompts for coding, review, diagnosis, and research tasks inside the Codex Claude Code plugin
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch
</system-reminder>
